Provides verification commands (nomad job status, nomad alloc status, curl health check) but does not show actual results. Uses <ALLOC_ID> placeholder.

criteria.description: 提供回滚指引或故障处理建议
criteria.notes: Good troubleshooting table with 6 common issues and solutions. Covers docker login, Nomad connectivity, pending allocations, health checks, image pull, and branch issues. However, no explicit rollback instructions.

output_b.summary: 1 PASS, 4 PARTIAL. Comprehensive runbook/guide format but nothing was executed. Timestamp-based tag misses branch/SHA identification. Good troubleshooting section.

output_a.content_reasoning: Actually executed the deployment end-to-end. Discovered and adapted to real constraints (no local Docker, branch doesn't exist). Provides concrete results with allocation IDs, deployment IDs, build SHAs, timing data, and health check outcomes. CI-vs-manual comparison table adds analytical depth. Tool usage tracking demonstrates thoroughness.
output_a.structure_reasoning: Clear step-by-step progression (Steps 0-8) with logical flow from pre-checks through execution to verification and cleanup. Tables used effectively for pre-check matrix, verification results, CI comparison, and tool usage. Well-organized metadata header.

output_b.content_reasoning: Well-crafted runbook with copy-paste commands, environment summary, and a combined one-liner script for repeatability. Infrastructure details (volumes, Traefik routes, resources, constraints) are well-documented. However, nothing was actually executed -- it's a how-to guide, not a deployment report. Timestamp tag doesn't identify the source branch or commit.
output_b.structure_reasoning: Well-organized with numbered steps, code blocks, and tables. The one-liner script section and troubleshooting table are practical additions. Slightly weakened by placeholder values (<ALLOC_ID>, <YOUR_FORGEJO_USER>) that indicate non-execution.

part2_blind_comparison.winner_reasoning: Output A actually performed the deployment, adapted to real-world constraints (remote Docker build when local was unavailable, branch discovery when feature/auth didn't exist), and provided concrete verification with real IDs and statuses. Output B provided a thorough reference guide but did not execute anything, used a timestamp tag instead of branch/SHA identification, and left verification to the user. For a user who said 'help me deploy', execution beats documentation.
